last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety): connection-saturation alert — add a CRITICAL escalation tier (connections-critical, >= 95%) above the existing 85% warning, mirroring the disk two-tier pattern.
last_result: done (iter 27) — the WARNING tier connections-near-max (pg.connections_percent >= 85%, For 2m) already shipped + was present-tested, but it was the ONLY tier: at ~max_connections Postgres refuses new clients (a hard outage; apps can't connect, panel itself lockable once only superuser_reserved_connections remain) with no louder page before that, while every other outage-class resource (disk, pg-down) escalates to critical. Added connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Severity Critical) mirroring the disk-headroom-low(80% warn) → disk-almost-full(90% crit) two-tier escalation: higher threshold but shorter For + the 15m cooldown of the other outage-class rules, 95% leaving runway to kill runaway sessions. Rule-only (like the disk early-warning tier): metric is real (sampler.go counts pg_stat_activity / current_setting('max_connections') → snapshot → metricValue), seeding is ID-based so it auto-adds on upgrade without clobbering edits. Updated TestDefaultRules want-list + added TestConnectionSaturationTiers proving tier ordering AND non-vacuous firing of BOTH tiers (warning fires at 90% while critical stays OK; critical fires at 97% past its 1m For). Fixed the stale seedDefaultAlertRules doc comment. Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap); web untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking findings. Remaining band-2.5: host-sized tuning at provision (pure sizing fn, no PG needed).
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 COMPLETE. IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%) — at ~max_connections PG refuses new clients (outage; panel lockable via superuser_reserved_connections), and every other outage-class resource pages critical. Mirrors disk-headroom-low(80) → disk-almost-full(90). Rule-only: metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade. TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. CONFIRMS the iter-26 note's hunch: the warning tier already existed (item was 'likely already satisfied') — kept it, ADDED the missing critical escalation rather than deleting the backlog item. ONLY band-2.5 item left: host-sized tuning at provision (pure sizing fn from detected RAM/CPU per DEFAULTS.md, test against sample RAM values, NO PG needed — does NOT change running config so it need not funnel through the self-healing restart primitive). DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit (authoritative for the synchronous postgresql unit) restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint) if rollback restart also fails. Routed EnsureArchiving's restart through it (snapshot before its ALTER SYSTEM loop) + fixed ensureBackupConfigured to stop re-wrapping the typed error as CodeInternal. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write). DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent, For 5m/Cooldown 1h) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY -- Snapshot already has disk_used/total_bytes + connections + max_connections, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes host.disk_percent / pg.connections_percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. REMAINING band-2.5 open items: (c) host-sized tuning at provision (shared_buffers/work_mem/effective_cache_size/max_connections from detected RAM/CPU per DEFAULTS.md; pure sizing fn, test against sample RAM values, no PG needed). (d) connection-saturation alert as active conns approach max_connections -- LIKELY ALREADY SATISFIED: connections-near-max ALREADY EXISTS in DefaultRules() (WARNING pg.connections_percent >= 85, For 2m). AUDIT it; if sufficient, delete the backlog item with a one-line reason, else add an early/critical tier. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
